message:
Extend subnet conversion inputs (#6076) ## 🔍 Problem `subnet()` only accepted CIDR strings, so pipelines with typed `ip` fields had to stringify values and assemble CIDR notation manually before converting them to subnets. ## 🛠️ Solution - Accept typed `ip` values, plain IP strings, and existing subnet values in `subnet()`. - Add optional row-wise prefix handling for string, IP, and subnet inputs. - Keep existing CIDR-string parsing and subnet passthrough behavior. - Use Tenzir vocabulary types (`Option`/`None`) for the new optional conversion flow. - Cover host defaults, dynamic prefixes, nulls, invalid inputs, and subnet inputs with integration tests. ## 💬 Review Focus on prefix normalization for IPv4 versus IPv6 and the null-return behavior for invalid strings or out-of-range prefixes. 📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#279
✅ Closes TNZ-510

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+title: IP address support in subnet
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+created: 2026-04-24T08:25:26.842453Z
+---
+
+The `subnet` function now accepts typed IP addresses, plain IP strings, and
+existing subnet values with an optional prefix length:
+
+```tql
+from {source_ip: 10.10.1.124}
+net = subnet(source_ip, 24)
+```
+
+This returns `10.10.1.0/24` without converting the IP address to a string first.
+When you omit the prefix, IPv4 addresses become `/32` host subnets and IPv6
+addresses become `/128` host subnets.

@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+from (
+  {
+    case: "bad-string",
+    result: subnet("not an ip", 24),
+    host: subnet("not an ip"),
+  },
+  {
+    case: "v4-too-wide",
+    result: subnet("10.1.2.3", 33),
+    host: subnet("10.1.2.3"),
+  },
+  {
+    case: "v6-too-wide",
+    result: subnet("2001:db8::1", 129),
+    host: subnet("2001:db8::1"),
+  },
+  {
+    case: "duplicate-prefix",
+    result: subnet("10.1.2.3/24", 16),
+    host: subnet("10.1.2.3/24"),
+  },
+  {
+    case: "null-input",
+    result: subnet(null.string(), 24),
+    host: subnet(null.string()),
+  },
+  {
+    case: "null-prefix",
+    result: subnet("10.1.2.3", null.int()),
+    host: subnet("10.1.2.3"),
+  },
+)
